d1e3.d: Unloading your film is just as easy as loading it!

d1e10.t: How to get your Film Developed
d1e10.d: There's already so much to consider when using a film camera, especially one like the Argus C3, so the concept of getting your film developed should be one that is easy and suits your current needs! The following sources utilize professional development equipment and allow you to mail in your film so you can get your memories developed no matter where you are!

d1e17.t: Introduction to the Argus C3 Camera
d1e17.d: This will serve as a guide to the Argus C3 rangefinder camera and theory in operation.

d1e24.t: Understanding the Exposure Triangle: Part 2 - Sunny 16
d1e24.d: Sunny 16 is a handy way to compensate for light with quickness when you're needing to take photos fast!

d1e31.t: How to Adjust Aperture and Shutter Speed settings
d1e31.d: This section deals with the adjusting your Aperture and Shutter Speed settings.

d1e39.t: Quick Overview of Film for your Camera
d1e39.d: There are many brands of film available for your Argus C3 but what are the types, film speed and why does it matter? This guide will offer a small overview.

d1e53.d: After having your desired aperture and shutter speed settings, you are now ready to take photos!

d1e60.t: Understanding the Exposure Triangle: Part 1 - Film Speed, Aperture, and Shutter Speed
d1e60.d: Understanding the connection between Film Speed, Aperture, and Shutter Speed, together known as the Exposure Triangle, will help you take properly exposed photos. Here is Part 1.

d1e67.t: Specs and Parts of your Argus C3 Camera
d1e67.d:
The Argus C3 is a range finder camera capable of taking photos on 35mm film. The following are the specifications and the different parts of your camera which will be further referred to in this manual.

  • Lens - 50mm Argus Cintar lens

  • Shutter Speeds from 1/10th of a second to 1/300ths of a second and Bulb setting

  • Aperture settings from f/3.5 to f/16.

  • Range from 3 feet to Infinity

  • A - Shutter Button

  • B - Range Finder Dial

  • C - Shutter Cocking Lever

  • D - Rangefinder Window

  • E - Viewfinder Window

  • F - Film Winding Knob

  • G - Shutter Speed Dial

  • H - Aperture Dial

  • I - Film Catch

  • J - Film Counter Dial

  • K - Bulb and Instantaneous Switch

  • L - Viewfinder Window

  • M - Rangefinder Window

  • N - Reminder Dial

d1e74.t: Tips on Framing and Depth of Field
d1e74.d: The 50mm Cintar is a good lens for just about any situation. Here's a quick guide on your lens and framing your shots.
